tasks.title: Auto-commit rally init and laps-hook install
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change git-hygiene, Phase 1 (tasks 1.1-1.3). Dependency #2 (tidy-rally-runtime-data-storage) is archived/merged and the workspace tracked-file set has been cleaned, so `summary.jsonl` is the only churning tracked data file.

Context: openspec/changes/git-hygiene/proposal.md, design.md (Decision 1), specs/git-hygiene/spec.md ("Setup auto-commit"). Sites: cmd/rally/main.go runInit (~L367) and the laps.InstallHooks call (~L203).

Outcome: After `rally init`, the staged setup files commit once as `rally: initialize workspace`. After laps-hook install, affected files commit as `rally: install laps hooks`. Both use `--no-verify` and commit ONLY when something is actually staged (re-running init on a clean repo is a no-op).

Files & scope: cmd/rally/main.go only. Reuse existing gitx helpers (GitOutput, GitRepoRoot, GitUserFallbackConfig); do not shell out raw. Do not redefine the gitignore or the tracked-file set.

Design / risk: MUST NOT sweep the user's pre-existing unstaged changes into the setup commit — stage only the specific paths setup created/modified and commit just those (`git commit -- <paths>` or a staged-set guard), never a blanket `git add -A`. Skip the commit when the staged set is empty. Tolerate non-git repos and git failures gracefully (warn, do not abort init). Write the stage+commit step as a small testable helper that returns whether it committed.

Acceptance: `go build ./...` passes; `rally init` in a fresh git repo creates exactly one setup commit and a second run creates none. Tests are the next lap. Do not tick tasks.md (VERIFY owns checkoff).

tasks.title: Tests: setup auto-commit behavior
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change git-hygiene, Phase 1 (tasks 1.4, 4.3, 4.4). Follows the previous lap's code in cmd/rally/main.go.

Outcome: Tests for setup auto-commit, following existing repo patterns (see internal/gitx/git_test.go for temp-git-repo style). Cover:
- init in a clean repo produces exactly one `rally: initialize workspace` commit (1.4).
- re-running init with nothing changed creates no commit (1.4).
- init in a dirty working tree (user has unrelated unstaged changes) does NOT commit those changes — assert they remain unstaged after init (4.3).
- laps-hook install produces a `rally: install laps hooks` commit; re-running hook install does not duplicate it (4.4).

Files & scope: test files only. Do not change production code; if a behavior is untestable, add a head lap describing the needed seam instead of forcing it.

Acceptance: `go test ./cmd/... ./internal/gitx/...` (and whatever package holds the new code) passes. Concurrent-`rally start` duplicate-commit coverage (part of 1.4) is best-effort; if it can't be made deterministic, say so in the wrapup rather than skipping silently. Do not tick tasks.md (VERIFY owns checkoff).

tasks.title: Lap-boundary commit instruction in done/handoff hook scripts
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change git-hygiene, Phase 2 (tasks 2.1-2.3). Spec: specs/git-hygiene/spec.md "Lap-boundary commit instruction".

Context: internal/laps/laps-done-hook.sh (13 lines) and internal/laps/laps-handoff-hook.sh (15 lines) emit wrapup guidance the agent reads after `laps done` / `laps handoff`.

Outcome: Each script's wrapup output additionally instructs the agent to commit its work:
- done: message form `<lap-description>: done`
- handoff: message form `<lap-description>: in progress (handoff)`
Keep it advisory and consistent with the existing `echo` lines. `<lap-description>` is a placeholder the agent fills with the current lap's description; phrase the instruction so that is clear.

Files & scope: the two hook scripts plus their fixture/assertion tests (internal/laps/hooks_test.go is the likely home — match its assertion style). Do not alter the audit-trail or `rally progress` lines. If these scripts are embedded (go:embed) and installed, confirm the embedded copy is what tests read.

Acceptance: tests assert each script's output contains the commit instruction with the correct message form (2.3); `go test ./internal/laps/...` passes. Do not tick tasks.md (VERIFY owns checkoff).

tasks.title: Leftover-work commit guidance at run start
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change git-hygiene, Phase 2 (tasks 2.4-2.5). Spec: specs/git-hygiene/spec.md "Leftover-work commit guidance"; design Decision 3.

Context: internal/relay/runner.go builds the agent prompt via agent.BuildPrompt(opts) (~L797); BuildPromptOptions lives in internal/agent (see TaskPrompt handling ~L791 and the conditional incompleteRetryGuidance pattern ~L790). gitx.IsWorkspaceDirty (internal/gitx/git.go:67) already excludes `.rally/` paths.

Outcome: At run start, when the working tree has uncommitted NON-rally changes (IsWorkspaceDirty == true), the initial prompt dynamically includes advisory guidance to review and commit those changes before starting assigned work — code changes MUST be committed (upfront or folded into the end-of-run commit); docs/config-only MAY be left. When the tree is clean (or only `.rally/` files dirty), the guidance is omitted entirely.

Design / risk: Follow the existing conditional-prompt-text pattern (cf. incompleteRetryGuidance). Thread the dirty-check result into BuildPromptOptions cleanly; keep the guidance string with the other agent_prompt snippets rather than concatenating in the wrong layer. Reuse IsWorkspaceDirty — do not write a second porcelain parser. Tolerate IsWorkspaceDirty errors (treat as clean / omit guidance, never crash the run).

Acceptance: `go build ./...` passes. Tests (2.5): dirty tree outside `.rally/` -> prompt contains the guidance; clean tree -> omitted; only `.rally/` files dirty -> omitted. Add to internal/relay/*_test.go following runner_test.go patterns. Do not tick tasks.md (VERIFY owns checkoff).

tasks.title: Verify phases 1-2 (setup commit + lap-boundary/leftover guidance)
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change git-hygiene. Verify the work so far (Phases 1-2): setup auto-commit, lap-boundary hook commit instructions, leftover-work run-start guidance.

Use the `openspec-verify-change` skill against change `git-hygiene`, then focus the report on tasks 1.1-1.4, 2.1-2.5, 4.3, 4.4.

Branch awareness: current branch is `dev`. Do NOT assume `main` is the merge target — determine it from the user, PR metadata, repo docs, or recent history, then diff with `git diff <target>...HEAD`. Identify the first lap/try of THIS batch; treat earlier branch work as pre-existing baseline (flag, do not remove). Do not rewrite git history.

Review with depth, not just green tests: confirm the setup commit stages only setup paths (open the commit, check it did not sweep user changes); `--no-verify` and the staged-set guard are present; both hook scripts emit the exact message forms; the leftover-work guidance actually toggles on dirty/clean/.rally-only trees (trace the prompt-assembly path). Report findings first; create head laps for substantive gaps; fix only tiny safe one-liners.

Check off the tasks.md boxes for the tasks you verified as correctly and thoroughly done; leave the rest unchecked and say why.

tasks.title: Fold rally state into work commit; retire CommitRallyState
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change git-hygiene, Phase 3 (tasks 3.1-3.4). Spec: specs/git-hygiene/spec.md "No standalone state commit"; design Decisions 4-5. Highest-risk lap — it touches the run-finalization git path.

Context: internal/relay/runner.go — autoCommit (L1438) already does `git add -A` then commits `rally: run N attempt M (harness)`; the CommitRallyState call is at L1128 in the finalization path. internal/gitx/git.go — CommitRallyState (L92) and rallyTrackedStatePaths (L162). (`summary.jsonl` is now the only churning tracked data file, so `git add -A` folds it into the work commit.)

Outcome:
1. Confirm autoCommit's `git add -A` stages the summary.jsonl append, so no standalone state commit is needed in the common path (3.1).
2. Remove the CommitRallyState call at runner.go:1128 (3.2).
3. Add the amend-fallback for no-code runs, inline at that call site (3.3): if nothing is staged/unstaged -> do nothing; else if HEAD's commit message has the `rally:` prefix -> amend HEAD, appending ` [+state]` to its message; else -> create a single `rally: update state` commit (no stacking). Read HEAD's message via `git log -1 --format=%s`.
4. Remove CommitRallyState and rallyTrackedStatePaths from gitx/git.go entirely (3.4); keep pathIsGitIgnored only if still used elsewhere (grep first).

Design / risk: Decide amend-vs-new by the `rally:` MESSAGE prefix, NOT author identity (GitUserFallbackConfig is only a fallback; with a configured user.name the rally commit uses the user's identity and would not match). The amend must preserve authorship and use `--no-verify`. Never amend a non-rally HEAD. Audit for other CommitRallyState callers before deleting (grep repo + tests).

Acceptance: `go build ./...` passes; no remaining references to CommitRallyState/rallyTrackedStatePaths (grep clean). Tests are the next lap. Do not tick tasks.md (VERIFY owns checkoff).

tasks.title: Tests: state-fold and amend-fallback scenarios
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change git-hygiene, Phase 3 (task 3.5). Separate test lap because the amend-fallback git-state behavior is subtle and deserves an independent, thorough pass.

Outcome: Tests over temp git repos asserting the finalization commit behavior:
- Code-producing run: ONE work commit (`rally: run N attempt M ...`) that includes the summary.jsonl append, and NO separate `rally: update state` commit.
- No-code run with rally-authored HEAD (message has `rally:` prefix): HEAD is amended and its message gains ` [+state]`; no new commit.
- No-code run with non-rally HEAD: a single `rally: update state` commit is created (and a second no-code finalization does not stack another).
- No changes at all at finalization: neither amend nor new commit; at most a debug log.

Files & scope: test files only (internal/relay and/or internal/gitx, matching existing finalization test patterns). Drive through the real finalization path where feasible; if you must test the amend-decision helper directly, keep that as a focused unit test too. If a scenario is hard to reach through the public path, add a head lap describing the needed seam rather than over-mocking.

Acceptance: `go test ./internal/relay/... ./internal/gitx/...` passes. Do not tick tasks.md (VERIFY owns checkoff).

tasks.title: Verify phase 3 (state fold / CommitRallyState retirement)
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change git-hygiene. Verify Phase 3 immediately — it is the single highest-risk slice (run-finalization git path).

Use the `openspec-verify-change` skill against change `git-hygiene`, then focus the report on tasks 3.1-3.5.

Branch awareness: current branch is `dev`; determine the real merge target (user / PR / repo docs / history) before `git diff <target>...HEAD`. Treat pre-batch branch work as baseline. Do not rewrite git history.

Review with depth: CommitRallyState and rallyTrackedStatePaths are fully removed with no dangling callers (grep); the amend-vs-new decision keys on the `rally:` message prefix (not author identity); amend uses `--no-verify`, preserves authorship, and appends ` [+state]` exactly once (no double-stacking across consecutive no-code runs); the no-changes path is a true no-op. Run the Phase 3 tests and a realistic smoke (a tiny scripted run if feasible); confirm a normal code run yields exactly one work commit containing summary.jsonl, `git status` is clean afterward, and no stray `rally: update state` commits appear in the common path.

Report findings first; create head laps for substantive gaps; fix only tiny one-liners. Check off the tasks.md boxes for the tasks you verified as correctly and thoroughly done.

tasks.title: Edge-case hardening tests (special chars, git unavailable)
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change git-hygiene, Phase 4 (tasks 4.5-4.6).

Outcome: Tests for the robustness edges of the new commit paths:
- Lap descriptions / commit messages containing special characters (quotes, newlines) are handled safely — no shell injection, no broken commit; the message is the literal content. Cover both the hook-script instruction form and any rally-side commit that embeds a description.
- git unavailable or repo-corrupted scenarios (not a git repo, or a failing git command) cause the new commit logic to log a warning and skip gracefully rather than crashing the run/init.

Files & scope: test files only, matching existing patterns (internal/gitx, internal/relay, cmd/rally as appropriate). For the git-unavailable case, prefer a non-git temp dir or a forced-failure seam over un-PATHing git globally.

Acceptance: `go test ./...` passes for the touched packages. If special-char handling reveals an actual production bug (e.g. a message built via unsafe string interpolation), add a head lap to fix it rather than weakening the test. Do not tick tasks.md (VERIFY owns checkoff).

tasks.title: Docs and version bump for git-hygiene
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change git-hygiene, Phase 4 (tasks 4.1, 4.7).

Outcome:
1. Document the new commit conventions in AGENTS.md and/or README.md (4.1): setup auto-commit (`rally: initialize workspace` / `rally: install laps hooks`), lap-boundary agent commits (`<lap-description>: done` / `: in progress (handoff)`), folded state (state rides the work commit; amend-fallback appends ` [+state]`; a single `rally: update state` only on a non-rally HEAD), and leftover-work run-start guidance. Keep it concise and consistent with the existing docs voice; place it where git/commit behavior is already discussed. Cross-check the wording against the implemented code, not just the proposal.
2. Bump internal/buildinfo/VERSION by one patch level (0.8.3 -> 0.8.4) per the release process in AGENTS.md (4.7). VERSION file only — do NOT create or push a git tag; auto-tag CI handles tagging on main.

Files & scope: AGENTS.md / README.md / internal/buildinfo/VERSION. No production logic changes.

Acceptance: docs accurately describe shipped behavior; `cat internal/buildinfo/VERSION` shows 0.8.4. Do not tick tasks.md (VERIFY owns checkoff).

tasks.title: Final full-change verification of git-hygiene
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change git-hygiene — final whole-change verification before archive.

Use the `openspec-verify-change` skill against change `git-hygiene` and run the COMPLETE verification; inspect the whole diff.

Branch awareness: current branch is `dev`. Determine the intended merge target (user / PR metadata / repo docs / recent history) — do not assume `main` — then review with `git diff <target>...HEAD`. Identify the first lap/try of this batch; earlier branch work is pre-existing baseline (flag, do not remove). Do not rewrite git history; for any scope cleanup use additive/revert commits or ask the user.

Verify all tasks are complete and coherent across phases. Run `go test ./...` and `go build ./...`.

Review with depth: confirm clean `git status` during/after a run, one commit per lap boundary where the agent complied, no standalone `rally: update state` in the common path, and accurate docs. For task 4.2 (tracked-file-set), verify against the ACTUAL tracked set in the tree rather than any stale list — note for the planner/user: tasks.md 4.2 names `.rally/instructions.md`, which does not exist in this repo, and the `agent_status.jsonl` churn was a pre-batch old-rally-version artifact cleaned up before this batch; flag any remaining mismatch rather than editing the OpenSpec docs yourself.

Report findings first; create head laps for substantive gaps; fix only tiny safe one-liners. Check off the tasks.md boxes for every task you verified as correctly and thoroughly done.

tasks.title: Width-aware single-line shortcut hint
tasks.description:
Context: cli-polish change, task §1 + design Decision 1. `ShortcutHint()` (internal/style/style.go:31) renders a fixed string ("Ctrl+S skip · Ctrl+P pause · Ctrl+X stop · Ctrl+C quit"). On a narrow terminal it wraps to two lines, which breaks the monitor's 1s cursor-up countdown redraw (the cursor-up line count is fixed at print time), so each tick appends instead of overwriting and stale status lines accumulate.

Outcome: `ShortcutHint` detects terminal width at render time and emits the widest of four tiers that fits on a single line, so the countdown redraw always overwrites cleanly.

Files & scope: internal/style/style.go (`ShortcutHint` + any local helpers) and internal/style/style_test.go. Do NOT touch the monitor redraw logic — guaranteeing one line is the whole fix.

Design: widest-that-fits among:
- Full: `[Ctrl+S skip] [Ctrl+P pause] [Ctrl+X stop] [Ctrl+C quit]`
- Medium: `[^S skip] [^P pause] [^X stop] [^C quit]`
- Narrow: `^S skip · ^P pause · ^X stop · ^C quit`
- Minimal: `^S·^P·^X·^C`
Use `term.GetSize(fd)` for width; one-shot detection at render time is sufficient (the tier never changes mid-run), so SIGWINCH handling is optional — skip it if it complicates testing. When stdout is not a TTY or detection fails, fall back to width 80 and never wrap. Measure visible width without ANSI styling. COORDINATION: keep the current action labels ("stop"/"quit"); the "graceful stop"/"quit now" relabel is owned by the later agent-lifecycle change — do not introduce it here.

Acceptance: style_test.go asserts each width band selects the expected tier and output is always ≤ one line. `go test ./internal/style/`. Report progress but do not check tasks.md boxes (VERIFY owns ticking).

tasks.title: Full-width headers and footers
tasks.description:
Context: cli-polish task §2 + design Decision 2. Headers/footers use fixed 40-char separators (style.go:44 `separatorWidth`, `separator()`/`subSeparator()`), so output is ragged and doesn't span the terminal.

Outcome: header/footer/summary separator lines fill the terminal width (capped at 80) with box-drawing fill, degrading gracefully on narrow terminals.

Files & scope: internal/style/style.go (`separator`/`subSeparator` and the `RenderHeader`/`RenderFooter`/summary paths) and style_test.go. Confine to style.go.

Design: replace the fixed `separatorWidth` with terminal-width-aware fill capped at 80. On terminals narrower than the label, clamp to the available width and truncate the label while keeping the line structure intact. Non-TTY/detection failure → width 80.

Acceptance: tests assert separators fill to the cap and clamp on narrow widths without breaking structure. `go test ./internal/style/`. Report progress; do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Bound activity age by try runtime
tasks.description:
Context: cli-polish task §5 + design Decision 4. `last activity` in `monitor.Tick()` is `time.Since(logMtime)` with no relation to when the current try started, so at a retry's start (elapsed≈0) it surfaces the stale previous-try mtime (e.g. "20h 50m ago") and the derived `⚠ slowing` indicator (≥0.6× the stall threshold) fires instantly and falsely.

Outcome: the displayed last-activity age can never exceed the current try's elapsed time. A just-started try reads `< 1m ago` regardless of the log file's pre-existing mtime, and `⚠ slowing` cannot appear until the try itself has been silent for ≥0.6× the threshold.

Files & scope: internal/monitor/monitor.go (the last-activity computation in `Tick()`) and monitor_test.go. Leave the stall detector in the reliability package untouched — only the *display* age is clamped, so liveness/kill behavior is unchanged.

Design: after computing `lastActivity`, clamp `if lastActivity > elapsed { lastActivity = elapsed }` using the monitor's known `startTime`, before deriving indicators. Leave the `lastActivity < 0` ("—") path unchanged.

Acceptance: test that at elapsed==0 with a stale mtime the age reads `< 1m ago` and `slowing` is absent until the try's own silence reaches 0.6× the threshold. `go test ./internal/monitor/`. Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Collapse retries into one updating line; colour only terminal outcome
tasks.description:
Context: cli-polish task §6 + design Decision 5. Each retry attempt prints its own red `✗ failed` footer (`RenderFooter`, around runner.go:1123), so an in-flight run that retries 5× shows five near-identical red lines and miscolours non-terminal attempts as failures.

Outcome: while a run is retrying within budget, a single neutral line updates in place (`↻ retrying N/M · last: <reason> (<dur>, <files>)`); exactly one coloured outcome footer prints at the terminal result — green `✓ passed on try N/M …` on recovery, red `✗ failed after K tries · <reason>` when the budget is exhausted. A single-attempt run (maxAttempts==1) is terminal on its first failure and coloured immediately.

Files & scope: internal/relay/runner.go (per-attempt footer orchestration around runner.go:1123), internal/style/style.go (`RenderFooter` gains a terminal-vs-interim notion), style_test.go, runner_test.go.

Design: suppress the per-attempt `RenderFooter` while retrying within budget; reuse the existing cursor-redraw mechanism for the in-place neutral line (safe — Rally does not currently print agent output inline). Add a terminal-vs-interim flag to `RenderFooter` so `FailureStyle` (red) is applied only to terminal failures; interim states use the neutral/dim style. Do not disturb the inline `retry N/M` live status indicator — this changes only footer cadence and colouring.

Acceptance: tests — a 5× retry prints one updating line + one final coloured footer (not five red footers); a single-attempt failure is red; a recovering run prints a green footer. `go test ./internal/style/ ./internal/relay/`. Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Verify display + retry UX slice
tasks.description:
Verify the user-visible display/monitor/retry slice (the four prior laps) for the cli-polish change — cli-display capability, tasks §1, §2, §5, §6.

Use the openspec-verify-change skill against change `cli-polish`, then focus the report on: width-aware shortcut hint tiers, full-width headers, try-runtime-bounded activity age, and the collapsed single-line retry + terminal-only colouring.

Before diffing, identify the intended merge target from the user / PR metadata / branch config / recent history — do NOT assume `main` — and identify the first lap/try in this batch; treat earlier branch work as pre-existing. Use `git diff <target>...HEAD`.

Depth: trace the actual rendered strings, not just green tests. Confirm `ShortcutHint` never exceeds one line across width bands, separators fill to the 80 cap and clamp on narrow widths, the activity-age clamp uses try elapsed (and the stall detector is untouched), and the footer path emits exactly one coloured line only at the terminal result. Confirm the current "stop"/"quit" labels were retained (the relabel is agent-lifecycle's job). Run `go test ./internal/style/ ./internal/monitor/ ./internal/relay/`.

Check off the tasks.md boxes under §1, §2, §5, §6 for work verified correct and thorough. For substantive gaps, add a focused head lap (`laps add head ...`) rather than fixing here; only tiny safe one-liners may be fixed in place. Do not rewrite git history.

tasks.title: Leftover-aware incomplete detection
tasks.description:
HIGH-RISK behavioral lap — relay-runner MODIFIED requirement (failure classification path).

Context: cli-polish task §7 + design Decision 6 + specs/relay-runner/spec.md delta ("Incomplete failure class"). Today the incomplete class (runner.go:986) is computed from the whole dirty working tree (`dirtyBeforeAutoCommit` plus `filesChangedList`'s `git status --porcelain` fallback at runner.go:1490), so uncommitted leftovers from a *prior* failed try make a later no-op try look incomplete.

Outcome: a try is "incomplete" only when *this* try produced its own uncommitted, unfinalized changes. Untouched leftovers inherited from a prior failed try no longer trigger the class; a try that touches (modifies/reverts/commits) an inherited leftover path has that path attributed to it.

Files & scope: internal/relay/runner.go — snapshot near the `headBefore` capture (runner.go:816); incomplete classification (runner.go:986); `filesChangedList` (runner.go:1490) — and runner_test.go.

Design: snapshot the already-dirty path set at try start (alongside `headBefore`) via `git status --porcelain`. Define "changes produced by this try" as the working-tree delta vs that snapshot (paths whose porcelain state differs before vs after). Base the incomplete classification on that delta, not the absolute dirty set. A try that both inherits leftovers and adds its own unfinalized changes is still incomplete. Preserve the incomplete-retry guidance flow and the resilience-cascade exemption (incomplete does NOT call `PauseAgent`/`RecordHourlyFailure`).

Acceptance: regression tests for all four spec scenarios — (a) a no-op try inheriting leftovers is NOT incomplete; (b) a try adding its own unfinalized changes IS; (c) touching/reverting/committing an inherited leftover attributes it to this try; (d) no-change-and-no-finalize stays a normal agent-class failure. `go test ./internal/relay/`. Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Verify incomplete-classification correctness
tasks.description:
High-risk follow-up to the leftover-aware incomplete lap.

Use the openspec-verify-change skill against change `cli-polish`, focused on the relay-runner MODIFIED "Incomplete failure class" requirement and its four scenarios.

Identify the intended merge target (do NOT assume `main`; use user / PR metadata / branch config / recent history) and the first lap/try in this batch before diffing; `git diff <target>...HEAD`.

Depth: trace the snapshot capture at try start, the before/after porcelain delta, and the classification call site — confirm untouched inherited leftovers are excluded and touched leftovers are attributed to the try. Verify the resilience-cascade exemption (no `PauseAgent`/`RecordHourlyFailure` for incomplete) and the incomplete-retry guidance string are intact. Run `go test ./internal/relay/`. Do not trust green tests alone — read the delta computation.

Check off tasks.md §7 boxes for verified-correct work. Substantive gaps → focused head lap; only tiny safe one-liners fixed in place. No git history rewriting.

tasks.title: rally init subcommands
tasks.description:
Context: cli-polish task §3 + design Decision 3. `rally init` does workspace init today; role init exists as a separate path.

Outcome: `rally init` = workspace init (unchanged), `rally init roles` = role init, `rally init all` = workspace + roles in sequence. Each subcommand is independently re-runnable (idempotent merge into existing config).

Files & scope: cmd/rally/main.go (init command wiring) plus the relevant main/cmd test.

Design: reuse the existing workspace-init and role-init code paths; `init all` runs them in order. Keep bare `init` behavior unchanged. Idempotent: re-running merges into existing config without clobbering.

Acceptance: tests — `init all` runs both in sequence; `init roles` only touches role configuration. Run the relevant package tests (e.g. `go test ./cmd/rally/`). Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Rename FallbackConfig to FreeRunPrompt with back-compat alias
tasks.description:
Context: cli-polish task §4 + design Decision 7. `FallbackConfig` only sets the task prompt for a laps-less, promptless ("free") run, but "fallback" reads like runner failover (a different mechanism). Pure naming/config clarity — no behavior change.

Outcome: type/methods/defaults and the config key are renamed to free-run naming; the old `[fallback] instructions_file` still loads (deprecated, warns) for one release; behavior is unchanged.

Files & scope: internal/config/config_v2.go (`FallbackConfig.InstructionsFile`→`FreeRunPromptFile`; alias in `LoadV2`), internal/relay/runner.go (`loadFallbackInstructions()`→`loadFreeRunPrompt()`, `builtInDefaultFallback`→`builtInDefaultFreeRunPrompt`), config_v2_test.go. Do NOT add a `[fallback]` section to the generated config template — it never had one; only deserialization (`LoadV2`) needs the alias.

Design: config key becomes `[free_run] prompt_file`. In `LoadV2`, accept the old `[fallback] instructions_file` as a deprecated alias, emit a deprecation warning when it is read, both resolving to the same value. The `loadFreeRunPrompt()` body and the `resolveRunTask()` call site are untouched (name-only).

Acceptance: tests — new key loads; old key still loads with a deprecation warning; both resolve to the same prompt. `go test ./internal/config/ ./internal/relay/`. Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Document init subcommands and free-run key; bump VERSION
tasks.description:
Context: cli-polish task §8.1 and §8.3.

Outcome: README/config docs document the `init` subcommands (`init`, `init roles`, `init all`) and the `[free_run] prompt_file` key with the one-release `[fallback]` deprecation note; the build version is bumped per the release process.

Files & scope: README.md (and any config docs) and internal/buildinfo/VERSION.

Design: document the three init subcommands and the free-run key plus its deprecation window. Bump the patch version in internal/buildinfo/VERSION — do NOT create or push a git tag (auto-tag handles tagging on main).

Acceptance: docs reflect the new commands and key; VERSION is incremented. Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Final full-change verification
tasks.description:
Whole-change verification for cli-polish.

Run the complete openspec-verify-change skill against change `cli-polish` and inspect the entire diff. Identify the intended merge target (do NOT assume `main`; use user / PR metadata / branch config / recent history) and the first lap/try of this batch; treat earlier branch work as pre-existing unless the user asks to include it. `git diff <target>...HEAD`.

Cover every capability: cli-display (hint tiers, full-width headers, activity-age clamp, collapsed retry + terminal-only colour), cli-config (init subcommands, FreeRunPrompt rename + back-compat alias), and the relay-runner MODIFIED incomplete class. Confirm the coordination note held — `ShortcutHint` kept the current "stop"/"quit" labels and left the relabel to agent-lifecycle. Run the full suite `go test ./...` and `go build ./...`; confirm the VERSION bump.

Check off any remaining tasks.md boxes (including §8) verified correct and thorough. Substantive gaps → focused head lap; only tiny safe one-liners fixed in place. Do not rewrite git history.

tasks.title: Graceful subprocess shutdown unified on SIGINT
tasks.description:
Context: openspec/changes/agent-lifecycle (proposal §1, design Decision 1, tasks 1.1-1.5). Cancellation today flows through exec.CommandContext, which SIGKILLs only the leader PID with no grace window; the stall killer already drains but on SIGTERM. Unify both kill paths on SIGINT-to-the-process-group.

Outcome: cancelling an attempt sends SIGINT to the whole process group and escalates to SIGKILL only after a bounded WaitDelay; the stall detector uses the same signal.

Files & scope: internal/agent/exec.go (SetProcessGroup: add Cmd.Cancel = syscall.Kill(-cmd.Process.Pid, SIGINT) and Cmd.WaitDelay = 5s); internal/reliability/freeze_unix.go:14 (map signalTerminate to SIGINT, not SIGTERM). Do not touch runner.go action handling here (that is the next lap). Grep all build-tag variants of sendProcessGroupSignal first — today only freeze_unix.go maps it; freeze_windows.go is an unsupported stub.

Design: keep the 5s drain consistent between the cancel path (WaitDelay) and the stall killer (processGroupKiller.drain). All executors already call SetProcessGroup, so this applies uniformly — confirm claude/antigravity/gemini/opencode/codex/generic.

Acceptance: a test asserts cancel signals the process GROUP (negative PID, -pid) with SIGINT, then SIGKILL after WaitDelay when the child ignores SIGINT — pin the group-reach win, not merely SIGINT-vs-SIGKILL. Existing internal/reliability/stall_test.go signal-sequence assertions (abstract signalTerminate then signalKill enums at :171,:204) still pass unchanged. go build ./... and go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/reliability/... green. Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Verify graceful shutdown + SIGINT unification
tasks.description:
Use the openspec-verify-change skill against agent-lifecycle, focused on the graceful-shutdown phase (tasks 1.1-1.5, spec 'Graceful subprocess shutdown').

Identify the intended merge target before diffing (ask the user / PR metadata / branch config / recent history — do not assume main), then diff <target>...HEAD. Treat commits before the first lap of this batch as pre-existing baseline.

Review depth: confirm Cmd.Cancel actually targets the negative PID (group), that WaitDelay is set, that freeze_unix.go now maps to SIGINT, and that no other build-tag variant of the signal mapping was missed. Trace that every executor still routes through SetProcessGroup. Run go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/reliability/.... Do not just trust green tests — read the kill path.

Check off the tasks.md boxes for 1.1-1.5 only for work you verified correct and thorough. Create a head lap (laps add head) for any substantive gap rather than fixing it here; only tiny safe one-liners may be fixed inline. Do not rewrite git history.

tasks.title: Cancel path must SIGKILL the process group after grace window
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change agent-lifecycle, graceful-shutdown slice follow-up from VERIFY. Current SetProcessGroup uses Cmd.Cancel=SIGINT to -pid plus Cmd.WaitDelay=5s, but Go's os/exec WaitDelay escalation only calls os.Process.Kill() on the leader and then closes parent pipes; it does NOT send SIGKILL to the process group. That leaves a spec gap versus graceful subprocess shutdown if a non-leader child ignores SIGINT, and the current exec_unix_test only proves group SIGINT reach + leader SIGKILL.

Outcome: make the cancel path escalate to SIGKILL for the whole process group after the 5s grace window, not just cmd.Process, while preserving the existing SIGINT-first behavior and bounded shutdown. Reuse a shared helper if it keeps the path coherent with internal/reliability/processGroupKiller. Handle already-exited groups cleanly (ESRCH should not surface as a spurious cancel error).

Tests: add a regression where both leader and inherited non-leader child ignore SIGINT; after the grace window both must be gone (no orphan child/process-group survivor). Keep the existing group-reach assertion too. Run go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/reliability/.... Do not tick tasks.md; VERIFY will re-check.

tasks.title: Cancel path must SIGKILL the process group after grace window
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change agent-lifecycle, graceful-shutdown slice follow-up from VERIFY. Current SetProcessGroup uses Cmd.Cancel=SIGINT to -pid plus Cmd.WaitDelay=5s, but Go's os/exec WaitDelay escalation only calls os.Process.Kill() on the leader and then closes parent pipes; it does NOT send SIGKILL to the process group. That leaves a spec gap versus graceful subprocess shutdown if a non-leader child ignores SIGINT, and the current exec_unix_test only proves group SIGINT reach + leader SIGKILL.

Outcome: make the cancel path escalate to SIGKILL for the whole process group after the 5s grace window, not just cmd.Process, while preserving the existing SIGINT-first behavior and bounded shutdown. Reuse a shared helper if it keeps the path coherent with internal/reliability/processGroupKiller. Handle already-exited groups cleanly (ESRCH should not surface as a spurious cancel error).

Tests: add a regression where both leader and inherited non-leader child ignore SIGINT; after the grace window both must be gone (no orphan child/process-group survivor). Keep the existing group-reach assertion too. Run go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/reliability/.... Do not tick tasks.md; VERIFY will re-check.

tasks.title: Verify cancel escalation kills the whole process group
tasks.description:
Follow-up VERIFY lap for agent-lifecycle graceful-shutdown tasks 1.1-1.5 after the cancel-path escalation fix lands.

Review depth: diff from the pre-fix baseline, read the actual kill path, and confirm the post-grace escalation is sent to the process group (not just cmd.Process). Re-run go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/reliability/.... Verify the stubborn-child regression proves no orphaned non-leader child survives after WaitDelay, and only then check off task 1.5 (and any other still-unchecked graceful-shutdown tasks that are now fully verified).

tasks.title: Make Ctrl+C quit-now immediate; keep Ctrl+X stop-after-try
tasks.description:
Context: design Decision 2, tasks 2.1-2.3, spec 'Responsive stop and quit shortcuts'. Today ActionStop and ActionQuit share one branch (internal/relay/runner.go:978-980) that only sets r.stopFlag and lets the current try run to completion — so stop/quit appear dead while an agent is stalled (the try ends only when the stall detector fires, up to ~180s).

Outcome: Ctrl+C cancels the running attempt immediately via graceful shutdown and aborts the relay; Ctrl+X keeps 'finish current try, then stop'; the action loop stays responsive during the cancel drain.

Files & scope: internal/relay/runner.go — split the shared branch in the in-try action loop (~:961-982). ActionQuit: set stopFlag, cancelAttempt(), drain tryCh, break actionLoop (mirror the pause/skip pattern). ActionStop: unchanged (set stopFlag, let try finish). While blocked on <-tryCh after cancelAttempt(), keep selecting on actionCh so a second Ctrl+C within the ≤5s WaitDelay escalates to an immediate SIGKILL of the process group. Surface a 'stopping…' indicator via the monitor/footer so the UI never looks frozen.

Design: this is concurrency-sensitive — avoid deadlocking the select loop or double-closing channels; the second-quit escalation must not race the WaitDelay SIGKILL. Reuse the existing cancelAttempt/tryCh machinery; do not invent a new cancellation channel. Leave the inter-run waitLoop branch (~:224) as-is (no active try there).

Acceptance: behavior is exercised by the next (test) lap; for this lap, manual reasoning + go build ./... green and existing runner tests pass. Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Tests for responsive stop/quit and drain escalation
tasks.description:
Context: task 2.4, spec scenarios under 'Responsive stop and quit shortcuts'. The in-try action loop is a select-based state machine, so its tests double as the design review for the previous lap.

Outcome: deterministic tests that pin the three behaviors.

Files & scope: internal/relay/runner_test.go (or a focused new test file). Drive the action loop with a fake executor/try channel and simulated keyboard.Action values.

Acceptance: (1) Ctrl+C cancels the running attempt and aborts the relay WITHOUT waiting for the try to finish; (2) Ctrl+X lets the current try finish then stops; (3) a stalled/long-running attempt ends promptly on Ctrl+C rather than waiting for the stall threshold; (4) a second Ctrl+C during the drain escalates (assert the force-kill path is taken). Use injected timeouts/fakes so tests are fast and deterministic — no real sleeps near WaitDelay. go test ./internal/relay/... green. Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Verify responsive stop/quit behavior
tasks.description:
Use the openspec-verify-change skill against agent-lifecycle, focused on the stop/quit phase (tasks 2.1-2.4, spec 'Responsive stop and quit shortcuts').

Identify the merge target before diffing (do not assume main). Review the actual select-loop edit in runner.go: confirm ActionQuit now cancels+drains+breaks and aborts the relay, ActionStop is unchanged, and the drain stays responsive to a second quit without deadlock or channel races. Confirm the 'stopping…' indicator is wired. Trace at least one test per scenario rather than trusting the suite count.

This is the highest-concurrency-risk slice: look specifically for goroutine leaks, double-cancel, or a hang when the child ignores SIGINT. Run go test ./internal/relay/... -race if feasible.

Check off tasks.md 2.1-2.4 only for verified-correct work. Head-lap any substantive gap; tiny one-liners only inline. No history rewriting.

tasks.title: Wire honest session resume for gemini, opencode, codex
tasks.description:
Context: design Decision 3 + the resume matrix table, tasks 3.1-3.4, spec 'Pause-now and honest session resume'. claude (--resume, claude.go:49) and antigravity (--conversation=, antigravity.go:80) wire the session correctly. gemini (gemini.go:54), opencode (opencode.go:60), and codex's main Execute (codex.go:173) return ResumeSupported()=true but never pass opts.ResumeSessionID — resume silently no-ops.

Outcome: a ResumeSupported()==true harness always passes its resume flag when ResumeSessionID is set, or honestly reports false.

Files & scope: internal/agent/gemini.go, opencode.go, codex.go. For each, confirm the CLI's real resume invocation against its --help, then append the resume flag when opts.ResumeSessionID != ""; codex should use 'exec resume <sessionID>' (the form already used by its liveness probe at codex.go:125-134) instead of a fresh 'exec'. If a CLI's resume cannot be verified, set that executor's ResumeSupported() to false instead of guessing a flag. Do NOT change the runner-side policy.

Design: preserve existing behavior — resume fires on any retry with a tracked session (runner.go:791-822) and FreshRestart clears it (runner.go:1157-1160); do not add a 'meaningful progress' heuristic. On resume failure, keep the existing degrade-to-fresh-try (do not error the run).

Acceptance: go build ./... green; tests in the next lap pin the contract. Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Verify responsive stop/quit behavior (recovery)
tasks.description: Queue-state recovery lap inserted because .rally/state/current_task.md still points at the already-completed stop/quit verify prompt while the real queue head has advanced to rall-37e0. Use this lap only to record the re-verification without consuming the unrelated resume-tests head task.

tasks.title: Resume contract + regression tests
tasks.description:
Context: tasks 3.5-3.6, spec scenarios under 'Pause-now and honest session resume'.

Outcome: tests that lock in honest resume across harnesses and guard the already-working pause/resume policy.

Files & scope: internal/agent/*_test.go for the contract test; internal/relay for the regression tests (follow existing test patterns; use the fixture executor where helpful).

Acceptance: (1) Contract test — for every executor, ResumeSupported()==true implies the built command args contain that harness's resume flag when ResumeSessionID is set (claude --resume, antigravity --conversation=, gemini/opencode/codex per their wired flag); executors reporting false are exempt. (2) Regression tests — pause captures the session ID into run-state; the next attempt reuses it; an explicit skip starts fresh; a FreshRestart strategy starts fresh. go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/relay/... green. Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Verify responsive stop/quit behavior (recovery)

Use this lap only to record the re-verification without consuming the unrelated resume head task.

tasks.title: Wire codex exec resume <sessionID> for honest resume (task 3.2)
tasks.description: codex.go ResumeSupported()=true but main Execute builds fresh 'exec' without resume, only the liveness probe uses 'exec resume'. Wire 'exec resume <sessionID>' into the main Execute args when ResumeSessionID is set, following the liveness probe pattern at codex.go:125-134. Ensure the report-file/output-schema flags still work alongside resume mode.

tasks.title: Add contract + regression tests for resume wiring (tasks 3.5, 3.6)
tasks.description: Task 3.5: For every executor where ResumeSupported()==true, assert the built command args contain the resume flag when ResumeSessionID is set. Already verified: claude, antigravity, opencode. Codex will be covered after work-ae43. Task 3.6: Add explicit pause-captures-session-ID, resume-reuses-on-next-attempt, explicit-skip-starts-fresh tests. Existing TestResumeRetryPassesSessionID and TestFreshStartRetryClearsRunState cover retry flows already.

tasks.title: Verify codex resume wiring + contract/regression tests (tasks 3.2, 3.5, 3.6)

Confirm: (1) all ResumeSupported()==true harnesses actually pass the resume flag when ResumeSessionID is set; (2) contract test covers all four resume-supporting harnesses; (3) regression tests cover pause-capture, resume-reuse, skip-fresh, FreshRestart-fresh. Run task 3.7 smoke pass (test-driving-rally pause/resume) if possible.

tasks.title: Verify honest resume + run pause/resume smoke
tasks.description:
Use the openspec-verify-change skill against agent-lifecycle, focused on the resume phase (tasks 3.1-3.6, spec 'Pause-now and honest session resume'), and perform task 3.7's smoke pass.

Identify the merge target before diffing (not assumed main). Smoke (task 3.7): run a test-driving-rally pause/resume pass (or, if a full relay isn't practical, a minimal real invocation) to confirm a paused run actually resumes the session on a resume-supporting harness rather than starting over. Report what you observed.

Check off tasks.md 3.1-3.7 only for verified work. Head-lap gaps; one-liners only inline. No history rewriting.

tasks.title: Rename shortcut labels to 'graceful stop' / 'quit now'
tasks.description:
Context: tasks 4.1-4.3, spec 'Shortcut label clarity'. The width-aware tier layout already shipped in cli-polish; only the label text changes here.

Outcome: the shortcut hint conveys timing — Ctrl+X 'graceful stop', Ctrl+C 'quit now' — at widths that fit.

Files & scope: internal/style/style.go:29-34 (shortcutHintTiers, four tiers indexed 0=full,1=medium,2=narrow,3=minimal). Tier 0 (full) carries 'graceful stop'/'quit now'; tiers 1-2 keep terse 'stop'/'quit' (already abbreviated-key, width-constrained); tier 3 (^S·^P·^X·^C) is unchanged. internal/style/style_test.go references all four tiers by index (:365-368) — update fixtures/assertions and confirm each tier still fits its width tier.

Acceptance: go test ./internal/style/... green; the full tier reads 'graceful stop' and 'quit now'; no tier overflows its width. Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Warn at relay start when a lane has a single runner
tasks.description:
Context: tasks 5.1-5.4, spec 'Single-runner lane warning'. R9: lane rotation already exists and keys off Benched/Exhausted (already landed in harden-relay-run-lifecycle); the gap is operator awareness when a lane has no fallback to rotate to.

Outcome: starting a relay warns the operator about any lane with exactly one runner entry.

Files & scope: internal/relay/route_runtime.go:130-145, where per-lane schedulers are built via routing.NewScheduler(resolvedEntries) per route. Detect len(resolvedEntries)==1 and emit a warning to the operator-facing sink (pick stderr or the monitor and be consistent). Add an encouraging note about multi-runner lanes to the relevant docs/defaults.

Design: warn, do not block — a single-runner lane is valid, just fragile. Do not duplicate the warning per try; emit once at relay start.

Acceptance: a test asserts a single-entry lane warns and a multi-entry lane does not (assert against the chosen sink). go test ./internal/relay/... green. Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Reconcile VERIFY role default-boundary spec to the doc
tasks.description:
Context: tasks 6.1-6.4, spec 'VERIFY role default boundary'. The embedded default role doc internal/agent_prompt/roles/verify.md ALREADY states the reporting-focused stance (trivial clearly-correct fixes allowed; substantial gaps → new head lap). This lap is reconciliation, not a behavior change.

Outcome: the spec language and the embedded doc agree; the generic doc stays OpenSpec-agnostic.

Files & scope: internal/agent_prompt/roles/verify.md (authoritative; .rally/agents/verify.md is the installed copy). Confirm it matches the spec scenarios; adjust wording only if they diverge — do NOT remove the small-fix allowance. Ensure no 'mark off tasks.md' instruction lives in rally core or the default doc. Confirm (read-only) that the OpenSpec-specific tasks.md-checkoff behavior is injected per-lap by prepare-laps only for laps with a related change, and cross-check the boundary rules in AGENTS.md.

Acceptance: doc and spec are consistent; no OpenSpec coupling leaked into the generic doc; go build ./... green. Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Verify labels, lane warning, and VERIFY-boundary slice

Confirm: the full shortcut tier emits 'graceful stop'/'quit now' and no tier overflows; the single-runner warning fires once at relay start on a one-entry lane and not on multi-entry lanes, at the chosen sink; the embedded verify.md and spec agree with the small-fix allowance intact and no OpenSpec coupling in the generic doc. Run go test ./internal/style/... ./internal/relay/....

Check off tasks.md 4.x/5.x/6.x for verified work. Head-lap gaps; one-liners only inline. No history rewriting.

tasks.title: Document lifecycle behavior and bump VERSION
tasks.description:
Context: tasks 7.1-7.4.

Outcome: README/AGENTS reflect the new lifecycle behavior, and the version is bumped per the release process.

Files & scope: README.md and/or AGENTS.md — document graceful stop vs quit now timing, pause-now/resume behavior, and the single-runner-lane warning. Note that the interactive shortcuts require a double-press within the confirm window (internal/keyboard), so a single press intentionally does nothing. internal/buildinfo/VERSION — bump the patch version (per AGENTS.md release process; do not create a git tag by hand). Keep label text consistent with cli-polish's tier layout.

Acceptance: docs match the shipped behavior and labels; VERSION incremented; go build ./... green. Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Final full-change verification of agent-lifecycle
tasks.description:
Run the complete openspec-verify-change pass against agent-lifecycle and inspect the whole diff.

Identify the intended merge target from the user / PR metadata / branch config / recent history (do not assume main); treat work before this batch's first lap as pre-existing baseline. Run go build ./... and the full go test ./... (with -race on internal/relay if feasible).

Verify the whole outcome end-to-end: cancel sends SIGINT to the group then SIGKILL; Ctrl+C aborts a running/stalled try immediately while Ctrl+X stops after the current try; every ResumeSupported() harness actually resumes; labels read 'graceful stop'/'quit now'; single-runner lanes warn; the VERIFY role doc/spec are consistent; docs and VERSION are updated. Trace the real call sites and emitted strings, not just test results.

Check off any remaining tasks.md boxes that are now verified complete. For any residual gap, add a focused head lap rather than fixing it here (tiny one-liners excepted). Do not rewrite git history; if scope cleanup is needed use additive/revert commits or ask the user for a recovery strategy.
